notes: Bridge map for model theorem -> executable conformance op evidence. The registry remains narrower than universal mechanized refinement, but `retarget_v1` is now backed by a machine-checked Go-trace contract instead of metadata-only baseline evidence. PV structural proofs added in ParallelEquivalence.lean (Q-PV-19).

critical_ops.contract_scope: Generated Go trace v1 rows with `op=retarget_v1`, replayed against Lean `PowV1.retargetV1` on matching `CV-POW` fixture ids.
limitations: This is a narrow trace-backed equivalence contract, not a universal proof for all possible retarget inputs.
Other critical ops remain baseline-only until they receive their own machine-checked bridge.
forkSelect models the runtime operation; equivalence with Go/Rust implementation is structural (same if/else chain), not proved via rfl.

Tie-break determinism for distinct block headers relies on block_hash_collision_resistance axiom (SHA3-256 collision resistance). This is a standard cryptographic assumption, not a Lean-provable fact.

critical_ops.contract_scope: Generated Go trace v1 rows with exact supported ids `CV-U-01, CV-U-02, CV-U-05, CV-U-06, CV-U-08, CV-U-09, CV-U-10, CV-U-11, CV-U-12, CV-U-13, CV-U-14, CV-U-15, CV-U-17` (implemented by `utxoApplyBasicExpectedIds` / `utxoApplyBasicBridgeRows`), replayed against Lean `applyNonCoinbaseTxBasic` on matching fixture ids.
limitations: This is a narrow trace-backed executable contract over the current supported replay subset, not a universal proof for all possible UTXO states or transaction inputs.
`CV-U-16` is intentionally excluded because it is a post-activation SLH row while the current formal `applyNonCoinbaseTxBasic` model on `main` remains pre-rotation.
The contract checks outcome, fee, and resulting UTXO count for the included replay rows; stronger universal state-equivalence theorems remain separate work.
The bridge now fails closed on id-set drift: if the supported UTXO replay ids change without updating `utxoApplyBasicExpectedIds`, the theorem and the repository-wide refinement checker both fail.

Per-error-code rejection on validateDASetIntegrity internals (duplicate commit, chunk hash mismatch, etc.) covered by conformance replay, not individual theorems, due to mutable RBMap state in do-block.
